Visualization

Visualization Module

Handles plotting and visualization of simulation results.

Uses matplotlib for real-time and saved plots.

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

0.8.0

class src.visualization.visualization.SimulationPlotter[source]

Bases: object

Real-time and post-simulation plotting.

Generates standard plots for: - Currents vs time - Voltages vs time - Speed vs time - Torque vs time - 3-phase relationships

static create_3phase_plot(history, figsize=(12, 8), grid_on=True, grid_spacing=None, minor_grid=False, grid_spacing_y=None)[source]

Create figure with 3-phase motor variables.

Parameters:

  • history (Dict[str, ndarray]) – History dictionary from SimulationEngine

  • figsize (tuple) – Figure size

Returns:

Matplotlib figure

Return type:

Figure

static create_current_plot(history, grid_on=True, grid_spacing=None, minor_grid=False, grid_spacing_y=None)[source]

Create dedicated current analysis plot.

Parameters:

  • history (Dict[str, ndarray]) – History dictionary

  • grid_on (bool) – Show grid

  • grid_spacing (Optional[float]) – X-axis spacing (s)

  • minor_grid (bool) – Show minor grid lines

  • grid_spacing_y (Optional[float]) – Y-axis spacing (A), 0 or None for auto

Returns:

Matplotlib figure

Return type:

Figure

static create_pfc_analysis_plot(history, figsize=(11, 9), grid_on=True, grid_spacing=None, minor_grid=False, grid_spacing_y=None)[source]

Create a dedicated PFC telemetry plot with PF, power, and command trends.

Return type:

Figure

static create_efficiency_analysis_plot(history, figsize=(11, 9), grid_on=True, grid_spacing=None, minor_grid=False, grid_spacing_y=None)[source]

Create a dedicated efficiency plot with input/output/loss power trends.

Return type:

Figure

static create_inverter_analysis_plot(history, figsize=(11, 11), grid_on=True, grid_spacing=None, minor_grid=False, grid_spacing_y=None)[source]

Create a dedicated inverter-realism telemetry plot.

Return type:

Figure

static create_multi_axis_plot(history, variables, figsize=(10, 6), grid_on=True, grid_spacing=None, minor_grid=False, grid_spacing_y=None)[source]

Create a multi-axis plot for an arbitrary list of variables.

Each variable will be plotted on its own y-axis to allow very different magnitudes to coexist (multi-scaling). Colors and legend are handled automatically.

Parameters:

  • history (Dict[str, ndarray]) – History dictionary from SimulationEngine

  • variables (list) – List of history keys to plot (must be in history)

  • figsize (tuple) – Figure size

  • grid_on (bool) – Show grid

  • grid_spacing (Optional[float]) – X-axis spacing (s)

  • minor_grid (bool) – Show minor grid lines

  • grid_spacing_y (Optional[float]) – Y-axis spacing, 0 or None for auto

Returns:

Matplotlib figure

Return type:

Figure

static create_measured_vs_true_current_plot(history, figsize=(12, 10), grid_on=True, grid_spacing=None, minor_grid=False, grid_spacing_y=None)[source]

Create a 4-panel overlay plot comparing measured vs true phase currents.

Requires currents_*_true keys in history (produced by SimulationEngine when a current-sense model is attached). Falls back gracefully when the true-current keys are absent.

Panels (top→bottom): 1. Phase A overlay (measured vs true) 2. Phase B overlay 3. Phase C overlay 4. Per-phase RMS error over time (rolling window of 100 samples)

Parameters:

  • history (Dict[str, ndarray]) – History dictionary from SimulationEngine.

  • figsize (tuple) – Figure size.

  • grid_on (bool) – Show major grid.

  • grid_spacing (Optional[float]) – X-axis major tick spacing [s], None for auto.

  • minor_grid (bool) – Show minor grid.

  • grid_spacing_y (Optional[float]) – Y-axis major tick spacing [A], None for auto.

Return type:

Figure

Returns:

Matplotlib figure.

static save_plot(fig, filepath, dpi=100)[source]

Save figure to file.

Parameters:

  • fig (Figure) – Matplotlib figure

  • filepath (Path) – Output path

  • dpi (int) – Resolution DPI

Return type:

None
